policy: External RSS is used only as source material. Publish short Czech own-words fast news with clear travel impact. No FAQ or mandatory image for fast updates.

items.title: Yellow Thunderstorm Warning Issued for 14 Bulgarian Regions on Tuesday
items.excerpt: A yellow weather warning for thunderstorms has been issued for 14 regions in central and eastern Bulgaria for Tuesday, July 21. The day will begin with sunny periods, but later again clouds with thunderstorm potential wi
items.originalTitle: Yellow Thunderstorm Warning Issued for 14 Bulgarian Regions on Tuesday
items.originalExcerpt: A yellow weather warning for thunderstorms has been issued for 14 regions in central and eastern Bulgaria for Tuesday, July 21. The day will begin with sunny periods, but later again clouds with thunderstorm potential will form. Short-lived rain showers and thunderstorms are expected, with the most widespread and intense activity likely in eastern areas and the mountains....

items.title: More Tourists, Fewer Beach Crowds: Sofia and Plovdiv Drive Bulgaria’s Summer Growth
items.excerpt: The number of tourists visiting Bulgaria this summer is increasing, but the growth is not concentrated in the traditional seaside destinations. According to Rumen Draganov, director of the Institute for Analysis and Asse
items.originalTitle: More Tourists, Fewer Beach Crowds: Sofia and Plovdiv Drive Bulgaria’s Summer Growth
items.originalExcerpt: The number of tourists visiting Bulgaria this summer is increasing, but the growth is not concentrated in the traditional seaside destinations. According to Rumen Draganov, director of the Institute for Analysis and Assessment in Tourism, tourist arrivals in the country have risen by around 2%, with the biggest interest currently focused on Sofia and Plovdiv. Draganov noted that the strongest periods for Bulgarian tourism are increasingly moving beyond the peak summer months and away from the Black Sea resorts....

items.title: Bulgaria Heads Toward First 40-Degree Heatwave of the Year at End of July
items.excerpt: Bulgaria is heading toward the hottest period of the year, with temperatures expected to reach around and above 40 degrees Celsius after July 25-26, according to early weather forecasts. The final week of July is likely
items.originalTitle: Bulgaria Heads Toward First 40-Degree Heatwave of the Year at End of July
items.originalExcerpt: Bulgaria is heading toward the hottest period of the year, with temperatures expected to reach around and above 40 degrees Celsius after July 25-26, according to early weather forecasts. The final week of July is likely to bring the highest temperatures recorded in the country since the beginning of the year. Forecasters expect a sharp transition toward prolonged sunny and extremely hot conditions after a period of unstable weather. ...

items.title: Bulgaria Rejects Forced Border Changes, Reaffirms EU and NATO Commitment
items.excerpt: Bulgaria will continue to view the European Union and NATO as the most suitable framework for protecting its national interests and will not accept a world based on spheres of influence or the use of force to redraw bord
items.originalTitle: Bulgaria Rejects Forced Border Changes, Reaffirms EU and NATO Commitment
items.originalExcerpt: Bulgaria will continue to view the European Union and NATO as the most suitable framework for protecting its national interests and will not accept a world based on spheres of influence or the use of force to redraw borders, Foreign Minister Velislava Petrova said at the opening of the Annual Conference of Bulgarian Ambassadors Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and Permanent Representatives in Sofia. Petrova said the government had received a clear mandate from Bulgarian citizens to end a period of uncertainty, move away from externally influenced foreign policy decisions and present the count
